name: Calanda Reservoir - Mas de las Matas Loop
name_original: Calanda y su embalse - circular por el Somontano del Sistema Ibérico
description: Cycle a moderate 42.4-mile (68.2 km) route around Calanda and its reservoir, exploring the scenic Somontano region of the Iberian System.
region_intro: Road cycling around Calanda offers diverse terrain across the province of Teruel in Aragon, Spain. The region features quiet roads, varied landscapes, and significant natural features like the Calanda Reservoir, which provides scenic routes. Cyclists can find a mix of terrain, from roads alongside the reservoir to challenging mountain passes within the nearby Maestrazgo region, characterized by its mountainous territory and low traffic. This area provides options for different cycling preferences, including demanding climbs with notable gradients. Best road cycling routes around Calanda The most popular road cycling route is Calanda and its reservoir - circular through the Somontano of the Iberian System, a 42.4 miles (68.2 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the scenic area around the Calanda Reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alcañiz – Castle of the Calatravos loop from Castelserás, a moderate 33.4 miles (53.7 km) path. This route features historical landmarks, including the Castle of the Calatravos, and passes through varied terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Morella – View of the Calanda Reservoir loop from Calanda, a 74.4 miles (119.7 km) trail leading through the wider region with views of the Calanda Reservoir, often completed in about 6 hours 4 minutes. Road cycling around Calanda is defined by the scenic Calanda Reservoir, quiet roads of the Maestrazgo region, and challenging mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult, longer rides. The routes in Calanda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 570 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Calanda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Calanda?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Calanda, offering a variety of experiences. These routes range from moderate loops around the scenic Calanda Reservoir to more challenging rides through the mountainous Maestrazgo region.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Calanda?
A: The region around Calanda offers diverse terrain. You'll find relatively flat roads around the Calanda Reservoir, ideal for scenic rides. For those seeking a challenge, the nearby Maestrazgo region features demanding mountain passes with significant gradients, such as San Cristobal (up to 19%) and Fortanete (up to 17%).

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Calanda?
A: Yes, there are a few easier road cycling routes around Calanda. While many routes offer moderate to difficult challenges, you can find paths that are less demanding, particularly those closer to the reservoir. Overall, 3 of the routes are rated as easy.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of the scenic Calanda Reservoir. For historical interest, consider routes that pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3635251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Calatravos&lt;/a&gt;. The charming villages of the Maestrazgo region also provide cultural points of interest. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38149478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcañiz – Castle of the Calatravos loop from Castelserás&lt;/a&gt; specifically features this historical landmark.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Calanda?
A: Calanda offers good road cycling conditions for much of the year. The region's climate is generally favorable, but for the most comfortable experience, spring and autumn are often ideal, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential chill of winter. The Maestrazgo region's higher elevations can be cooler.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Calanda?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Calanda are circular. A popular example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958864952/calanda-and-its-reservoir-circular-through-the-somontano-of-the-iberian-system" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calanda and its reservoir - circular through the Somontano of the Iberian System&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 42.4-mile loop exploring the reservoir area.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Calanda?
A: The road cycling routes in Calanda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Cyclists often praise the quiet roads, varied landscapes, and the mix of scenic reservoir routes with challenging mountain passes in the Maestrazgo region.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. The Maestrazgo region, near Calanda, is known for its challenging mountain passes. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morella – View of the Calanda Reservoir loop from Calanda&lt;/a&gt;, a 74.4-mile trail with significant elevation gain, offer demanding climbs and are rated as difficult. Overall, 11 routes are categorized as difficult.

Q: Is it possible to find accommodation or places to eat that are cyclist-friendly in Calanda?
A: Calanda and the surrounding villages in the Maestrazgo region are generally welcoming to outdoor enthusiasts. You can find various accommodations and local eateries. It's advisable to check in advance for specific cyclist-friendly services like secure bike storage.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with other outdoor activities in the Calanda area?
A: Yes, the region offers opportunities for other outdoor activities. Besides road cycling, you can explore hiking trails, including routes around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5821865" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estanca de Alcañiz&lt;/a&gt;. The natural scenery and historical villages also provide a rich cultural experience.

Q: Are there any routes that offer extensive views of the Calanda Reservoir?
A: Yes, several routes provide excellent views of the Calanda Reservoir. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958864952/calanda-and-its-reservoir-circular-through-the-somontano-of-the-iberian-system" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calanda and its reservoir - circular through the Somontano of the Iberian System&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to explore this scenic area, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morella – View of the Calanda Reservoir loop from Calanda&lt;/a&gt; also offers expansive views.

Q: What is the typical duration for road cycling routes in Calanda?
A: The duration of road cycling routes in Calanda varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958864952/calanda-and-its-reservoir-circular-through-the-somontano-of-the-iberian-system" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calanda and its reservoir - circular through the Somontano of the Iberian System&lt;/a&gt; can take around 3 hours, while more difficult and longer routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morella – View of the Calanda Reservoir loop from Calanda&lt;/a&gt;, may require over 6 hours to complete.
tips: Mas de las Matas is a small town in the region of Baix Aragó that is right at the foot of the Iberian System. Surrounded by limestone mountains such as the Sierra de los Caballos
Road that runs by the Calanda reservoir and where the white limestone causes a turquoise blue in the dam. The road connects Calanda with Mas de las Matas.

The Church of Our Lady of Hope in Calanda (Teruel) is a prominent Baroque church with a rich history dating back to the 13th century. The original building was a small church constructed in the late 13th century. In 1462, Pedro Vacca, Lord of Calanda, rebuilt and expanded it, linking it to the Order of Calatrava.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Calanda and its reservoir circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one, so you can typically start and park in one of the main settlements along the way. Calanda itself, or Mas de las Matas, are good options with available parking, allowing you to easily access the loop.

faqs.question: Is the Calanda and its reservoir circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling. While dogs are generally welcome in many outdoor areas in Spain, bringing a dog along on a road bike route of this length and difficulty is not practical or safe. It's best to leave your canine companion at home for this specific cycling adventure.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Calanda and its reservoir route?
faqs.answer: The Somontano region, where this route is located, offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring and autumn. These seasons provide milder temperatures, which are ideal for a moderate 68 km ride with significant elevation gain. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures, making spring and autumn the most comfortable times.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads through the Somontano region of the Iberian System.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' and involves an elevation gain of approximately 870 meters over 68 kilometers. While the surfaces are generally well-paved, the distance and climbing demand a good level of fitness. It might be challenging for absolute beginners, but those with some road cycling experience and a decent fitness level should find it enjoyable.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows well-paved, easy-to-ride secondary roads. The Teruel province is known for its good asphalt and low traffic on these types of roads, making for a pleasant cycling experience. However, be prepared for some challenging sections due to the significant elevation gain.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights along the Calanda and its reservoir circular route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the charming settlements of Calanda and Mas de las Matas, which offer glimpses into local life. A central feature of the route is the View of the Calanda Reservoir, providing picturesque scenery.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Calanda and its reservoir circular route?
faqs.answer: On average, cyclists can expect to complete this 68-kilometer route in approximately 3 hours and 16 minutes. This duration can vary based on your cycling speed, fitness level, and how often you stop to enjoy the views or visit the villages.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily a standalone circular route, it does briefly intersect with sections of other official trails. These include parts of La Ginebrosa - Punta Tarayola - río Bergantes - Las Masadetas - Tozal de Catma - La Ginebrosa, Tierra de Contrastes, and Camino del Maestrazgo-Bajo Aragón, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through settlements like Calanda and Mas de las Matas, you will find opportunities for refreshments, meals, and potentially accommodation. These villages are ideal spots to refuel and experience local culture.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and views can I expect while cycling this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse landscape characteristic of the Somontano region. This includes areas of natural beauty, charming villages where time seems to stand still, and the prominent Calanda Reservoir, which offers picturesque views. The region is known for its contrasts, from rugged areas to expansive plains.
